name: Eddy's Oyster Loaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Almost split the loaves lengthwise, leaving a hinge. Scoop out the soft middle and save the crumbs.
2. Mix 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder with 2 tablespoons melted butter.
3. Brush the cavities of the loaves with the garlic-butter mixture.
4. Drain the oysters and save the liquid. Sauté the oysters in the remaining butter for about 5 minutes until the edges curl.
5. Add the sautéed oysters and saved oyster liquid to the loaves.
6. Close up the loaves.
7. Wrap them in cheesecloth dipped in milk, twisting the ends and tucking under the loaf.
8. Bake on a baking sheet for 30 minutes at 350°F.
9. Cut into halves before serving.
6 servings
(Note from the book: Eddy was a San Franciscan who owned a bar and made small oyster loaves for his married male customers to take home to their wives. These were pacifiers. But Eddy is dead now, and Mother must pacify herself. This is good for a special lunch. I also know a family who has it traditionally as Christmas Eve supper.)
